name: Midazolam
description: Short-acting benzodiazepine with rapid onset. Preferred for procedural sedation, RSI pre-medication, and IM/IN seizure management. IM and intranasal routes provide significant advantage in field settings.
dose: Sedation: 0.05-0.1 mg/kg IV titrated. Procedural: 1-5 mg IV slow push. IM: 0.2 mg/kg. IN: 0.2 mg/kg (max 10 mg). Status epilepticus IM: 10 mg IM (adult).
contraindications: Acute alcohol intoxication with cardiovascular compromise
warnings: Respiratory depression -- monitor airway, have reversal agent (flumazenil) if available.
Rapid IV injection causes apnea -- push slowly.

pediatric.dose: Sedation: 0.05-0.1 mg/kg IV. IM: 0.1-0.2 mg/kg. IN: 0.2-0.3 mg/kg. Seizure IM: 0.2 mg/kg (max 10 mg).
pediatric.note: IN route particularly useful in children -- avoids IV access.

obstetric.note: Crosses placenta. Neonatal sedation possible. Use minimum effective dose; monitor neonate if given near delivery.

source: JTS CPG / Standard Procedural Sedation Guidelines
